case_subject: Clubcard promotion triggered Gmail rate limiting
case_description: Gmail delivery slowed after a Clubcard promotion expanded above Tesco's established daily volume baseline.
root_cause_summary: A rapid volume increase on the promotional IP caused Gmail 421 temporary deferrals and slower delivery on repeat attempts; authentication remained aligned.
resolution_summary: Recovery in progress under a reduced Gmail volume cap.

case_subject: Microsoft retry queue delaying Nectar offers
case_description: Outlook and Hotmail messages remain in Amazon SES retries after a Nectar bonus-points campaign.
root_cause_summary: Microsoft temporarily suspended the sending IP after sustained high throughput, creating a retry backlog and high subsequent-attempt latency.
resolution_summary: Escalated while the Microsoft queue clears and reputation recovers.

case_subject: Dormant win-back audience caused invalid-recipient spike
case_description: A 365-day win-back campaign produced a sharp rise in invalid-recipient hard bounces across several mailbox providers.
root_cause_summary: The audience included old addresses without recent delivery or account activity, exposing stale and mistyped recipient records.
resolution_summary: Dormant records were excluded and future win-back eligibility was reduced to recently active profiles.

case_subject: Yahoo deferrals increased after promotional frequency doubled
case_description: Yahoo and AOL delivery slowed after More Card promotional frequency increased from three sends per week to daily activity.
root_cause_summary: The higher frequency and expansion into lower-engagement users weakened Yahoo reputation signals and increased temporary deferrals. Authentication remained healthy.
resolution_summary: Deliverability guidance has been provided; the customer must reduce frequency and execute a controlled engaged-audience recovery.

resolution_summary: Deliverability supplied a revised conservative warming plan; Customer Email Ops owns volume configuration and execution.

case_subject: Yahoo flash-sale burst caused temporary deferrals
case_description: A short-duration Lidl Plus flash-sale send released most Yahoo volume within twenty minutes and triggered sustained temporary deferrals.
root_cause_summary: The total daily volume was supportable, but the delivery speed exceeded the established Yahoo throughput pattern and created a retry queue.
resolution_summary: Deliverability identified the burst-rate issue and supplied a Yahoo speed cap; Customer Email Ops applied the cap and delivery recovered.

case_subject: Yahoo complaints increased after consent migration
case_description: Yahoo feedback-loop complaints increased after older preference records were migrated into the new promotional eligibility table.

case_subject: iCloud mailbox-full soft bounces delayed member prices
case_description: iCloud and me.com recipients showed an unusual increase in mailbox-full responses and delayed delivery during a member-prices send.
root_cause_summary: Apple temporarily rejected a higher-than-normal proportion of recipient mailboxes as over quota; SparkPost retries extended delivery without indicating an IP block.
resolution_summary: Monitoring retries; no sender-side block remediation is currently required.

case_subject: Spam-trap hits followed an old competition-list import
case_description: Microsoft monitoring reported spam-trap activity after Iceland added a historical competition audience to a Bonus Card promotion.
root_cause_summary: The imported audience contained old, unverified addresses with no recent relationship or successful delivery history, increasing trap and invalid-recipient risk.
resolution_summary: Deliverability isolated the risky source and recommended exclusion; Customer Data Operations removed the audience and tightened acquisition controls.

case_subject: Gmail inbox placement declined after promotional over-mailing
case_description: M&S Food increased promotional frequency across overlapping Dine In audiences, followed by weaker Gmail engagement and increased spam-folder placement.
root_cause_summary: Recipients received overlapping campaigns with limited suppression between sends, reducing engagement and weakening Gmail domain reputation.
resolution_summary: Deliverability defined an engaged-audience recovery plan; Customer Email Ops reduced frequency and implemented cross-campaign suppression.

case_subject: Out-of-band bounces increased on Smart Pass reactivation
case_description: Gmail accepted Smart Pass reactivation mail initially but later returned an elevated number of out-of-band bounces.
root_cause_summary: Older acquisition cohorts contained stale addresses that passed initial SMTP acceptance but were later rejected as invalid or disabled.

root_cause_summary: The expanded cohort had no recent opens, clicks or purchases, weakening engagement signals and increasing spam-folder placement despite stable acceptance.
resolution_summary: Audience segmentation has been revised; a recovery test is pending.

case_subject: Re-engagement marketing degraded a transactional IP
case_description: Heron sent a broad promotional re-engagement campaign through an IP previously used for steady transactional messages.
root_cause_summary: The promotional volume spike and low-engagement audience changed the traffic profile of the transactional IP, increasing Microsoft blocks and complaints.
resolution_summary: Deliverability recommended strict traffic separation and a reputation-recovery plan; Customer Email Ops owns routing and campaign changes.

case_subject: Shared IP pool caused cross-region Microsoft blocks
case_description: Several regional SPAR subaccounts received the same Microsoft block responses because they shared one promotional IP pool.
root_cause_summary: A high-complaint regional campaign degraded the shared pool reputation, affecting otherwise healthy regional subaccounts using the same sending IP.
resolution_summary: Regional traffic is being separated while the shared pool reputation recovers.
